steps.title: Use a simple scale
steps.content: Because every listed $x$-value contains the same constant $s$, set $s=1$ to see the pattern with representative points $(-2,24)$, $(-1,21)$, and $(1,15)$.

steps.title: Find the representative line
steps.content:
Use two points to find the slope:
$$
\frac{21-24}{-1-(-2)}=-3
$$
With point $(-1,21)$ in $y=-3x+b$,
$$
21=3+b
$$
$$
b=18
$$
So the representative line is $y=-3x+18$.

steps.title: Restore the scale
steps.content: Undoing the temporary choice $s=1$ makes the slope $-\frac{3}{s}$ while the intercept stays $18$: $$ y=-\frac{3}{s}x+18 $$ $$ sy=-3x+18s $$ $$ 3x+sy=18s $$ The answer is B.
